summary: Terms acceptance feature fully tested - all tests passed. Backend endpoints (POST /api/auth/accept-terms, GET /api/auth/me with terms_accepted status) working correctly. Frontend terms acceptance modal appears for unauthenticated users, checkbox must be checked before Continue button works, and after acceptance user is redirected to dashboard. Landing page and Terms page render correctly with all required sections.
passed_tests: GET /api/auth/me returns terms_accepted: false for new user
POST /api/auth/accept-terms accepts terms and returns success
GET /api/auth/me returns terms_accepted: true after accepting
Existing user with terms already accepted - GET /api/auth/me returns terms_accepted: true
Landing page loads with updated disclaimer section
Landing page has 'User Responsibility' in disclaimer
Footer has Terms of Service link pointing to /terms
Footer has Privacy Policy link pointing to /terms
/terms page loads and displays all 14 sections
Terms acceptance modal appears for new users accessing /dashboard
Terms modal shows: Not Legal Advice, AI Disclaimer, User Responsibility, Confidentiality Warning, Privacy & Data sections
Terms acceptance checkbox is visible with data-testid='accept-terms-checkbox'
Accept button is visible with data-testid='accept-terms-btn'
Accept button is disabled when checkbox is not checked
Accept button enables when checkbox is checked
After accepting terms, user redirects to dashboard
User with accepted terms goes directly to dashboard (no modal)

landing_page_disclaimer.details: Updated disclaimer includes 'User Responsibility' section explaining users are responsible for ensuring permissions to upload documents

terms_of_service_page.details: /terms page displays all 14 sections including Introduction, Service Description, Not Legal Advice, AI Disclaimer, User Responsibilities, Confidentiality Warning, Limitation of Liability, Indemnification, Privacy Policy, Data Retention, Governing Law, Changes to Terms, Contact, Acknowledgment

footer_links.details: Footer contains both 'Terms of Service' and 'Privacy Policy' links pointing to /terms

terms_acceptance_modal.details: Modal appears for users with terms_accepted: false when accessing protected routes. Shows all required sections, checkbox must be checked to enable Continue button

post_accept_terms_api.details: POST /api/auth/accept-terms updates user's terms_accepted to true and sets terms_accepted_at timestamp

get_auth_me_api.details: GET /api/auth/me returns terms_accepted and terms_accepted_at fields

protected_route_flow.details: ProtectedRoute component checks terms_accepted status and shows TermsAcceptance modal if not accepted

critical_code_review_comments: Terms acceptance implementation follows best practices with proper state management
Backend properly stores terms_accepted_at timestamp with timezone info
Frontend uses data-testid attributes for testability
Protected routes correctly check terms_accepted status before rendering children
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_terms_acceptance.py - Created comprehensive test file for terms acceptance

test_credentials.note: Test users created dynamically with cleanup fixtures

seed_data_creation: Dynamic test user creation with automatic cleanup in pytest fixtures
context_for_next_testing_agent: Terms acceptance feature fully implemented and tested. All 5 backend tests pass. Frontend flow works: new user sees modal → checks checkbox → clicks accept → redirects to dashboard. User with accepted terms bypasses modal. Test file at /app/backend/tests/test_terms_acceptance.py can be reused.
